etymology.description: phonetic 生 (shēng, "life") + phonetic 井 (jǐng, "well") -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character 青(qīng, "green") originally combines the phonetic components 生(shēng, "life") and 井(jǐng, "well") for their sound values. The ancient interpretation associated 青(qīng) with the color of growing plants, possibly influenced by the idea of cinnabar for dyeing, reflecting a bluish-green shade common in nature. This phonetic pairing with 生 and 井 suggests a pronunciation that fits the word's meaning.

examples.chinese: 树叶是青色的(樹葉是青色的)。
青山环绕着村庄(青山環繞著村莊)。
她有一双青色的眼睛(她有一雙青色的眼睛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī shuāng qīngsè de yǎnjīng.
examples.english: She has a pair of green eyes.
